info.name: Databricks REST API
info.description:
The Databricks REST API provides programmatic access to manage Databricks workspace resources including clusters, jobs, and workspace objects. All API endpoints require authentication using a personal access token or OAuth token passed via the Authorization header. The base URL is specific to your Databricks workspace deployment region.

Contact Support:
 Name: Databricks
 Email: support@databricks.com

request.description: Creates a new Spark cluster. This method acquires new instances from the cloud provider and starts the Spark driver and worker processes. The cluster is created asynchronously; use the cluster_id returned to poll for status.

response.name: Cluster creation initiated successfully.

request.description: Permanently deletes a Spark cluster. If the cluster is running, it is terminated and resources are asynchronously removed. If the cluster is terminated, it is immediately removed. A cluster can only be permanently deleted by an admin or the cluster creator.

response.name: Cluster permanently deleted.

query.description: (Required) The absolute path of the workspace directory to list. Must start with /.
If provided, only notebooks modified after this timestamp (epoch seconds) are returned.

request.description: Lists the contents of a directory in the workspace, or the object if it is not a directory. If the input path does not exist, a RESOURCE_DOES_NOT_EXIST error is returned.

response.name: Successfully listed workspace objects.

request.description: Creates a new job with the provided settings. The job can be configured with a single task or multiple tasks with dependencies forming a directed acyclic graph (DAG).

response.name: Job created successfully.

query.description: The number of jobs to return. This value must be greater than 0 and less than or equal to 25. The default value is 20.
The offset of the first job to return.
A filter on the list based on the exact (case-insensitive) job name.
Whether to include task and cluster details in the response.

query.description: The job for which to list runs. If omitted, lists runs for all jobs.
If true, only active runs are included in the results; otherwise lists both active and completed runs.
If true, only completed runs are included in the results.
The offset of the first run to return.
The number of runs to return (max 25).
The type of runs to return.
Whether to include task details in each run.
Filter runs started after this time (epoch milliseconds).
Filter runs started before this time (epoch milliseconds).
